x-enumeration-literals.name: KYC Assessment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Details about the customer being evaluated.

Includes:
Customer identification
Customer type (individual, business)
Financial profile
Income or revenue information

Example:
A bank evaluates an individual earning €4,000 per month applying for a loan.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Credit Request Assessment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Information about the credit plan requested by the customer.

Includes:
Requested credit amount
Credit product type (loan, credit card, overdraft)
Requested term or duration
Purpose of the credit

Example:
Customer requests a €15,000 personal loan for home renovation.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Financial Evaluation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Assessment of the customer's ability to repay the credit.

Includes:
Income analysis
Debt-to-income ratio
Cash flow evaluation
Existing liabilities

Example:
The borrower has existing loan payments of €500 monthly.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Creditworthiness Analysis
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Evaluation of the customer's past credit behavior.

Includes:
Credit score
Credit history
Past repayment performance
Default or delinquency records

Example:
A customer with a credit score of 720 and no missed payments.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Risk Assessment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Determines the risk level of granting the credit plan.

Includes:
Risk rating (low, medium, high)
Probability of default
Internal risk model results

Example:
A stable salaried employee is classified as low risk.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Collateral Assessment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Evaluation of assets pledged to secure the credit.

Includes:
Collateral type (property, vehicle, securities)
Collateral market value
Loan-to-value ratio

Example:
A house valued at €300,000 securing a €240,000 mortgage.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Compliance Check
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Ensures the credit plan meets legal and regulatory requirements.

Includes:
KYC verification
Anti-money laundering (AML) checks
Internal policy compliance

Example:
The bank confirms the customer identity and legal eligibility.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Credit Limit Determination
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines the amount of credit the bank is willing to approve.

Includes:
Maximum allowable credit
Approved credit amount
Credit exposure limits

Example:
Requested €20,000 but approved €15,000.
